channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Strategic alignment isn’t enough for a merger to succeed
item.description: Opinion: While the synergies may look compelling, it’s easy to miss the real challenge in a nonprofit merger — the human factor.

item.title: Stockholm Struggles to Find Words Over Diplomat in Epstein Files
item.description: The offices of Sweden’s mission to the United Nations in New York City and in Geneva are keeping mum as questions arise about the involvement of Lisa Svensson, a top Swedish diplomat, with the disgraced American billionaire Jeffrey Epstein. Marie Schram, a counselor at the permanent mission of Sweden in Geneva, where Svensson works as ... Read more

item.title: Higher ed and social enterprise are critical to address the USAID vacuum
item.description: Opinion: From the U.S. Feed the Future Innovation Labs to university incubators, academia and social enterprise are vital to the new global development framework.

item.title: Devex Pro Insider: The messy final days of USAID
item.description: Shutting down the world’s largest bilateral aid agency has gone from daunting to dysfunctional.

item.title: The top global development employers of 2025
item.description: Devex looks at hiring trends for one of the most turbulent years for job seekers in the world of global development.

item.title: Legal opinion warns development banks may violate climate law
item.description: The opinion argues major multilateral lenders — and their shareholder governments — risk violating international law if they continue financing fossil fuel projects.

item.title: How to land your next mission-driven role, from someone who's done it
item.description: This former USAID staffer found meaningful and purpose-driven work outside of the global development sector.

item.title: Devex CheckUp: Washington’s chosen faith-based org
item.description: America's new global health strategy cuts U.S. support to local organizations to fight HIV, except for FBOs. Plus, U.S. funding halted for NGOs in Somalia, and South Africa's plans to manufacture lenacapavir locally.

item.title: Devex Newswire: USAID challenged deadly gender norms in Nepal. No more
item.description: Old, deadly norms that undervalued the lives of women and girls have resurfaced following the demise of a USAID-funded program in Nepal. Plus, the details of U.S. critical minerals plan Project Vault.

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Further escalation drives uncertainty and suffering
item.description: On day six of the war in the Middle East, there's been no let-up in bombs, drones and rockets targeting Iran, Israel, Lebanon and many Gulf States, while NATO forces reportedly intercepted a missile fired at Turkïye by Iran, a claim Tehran denied. We'll bring you the latest from the United Nations and our partners at headquarters and in the field. Thank you for joining us. UN News app users can follow the coverage here

item.title: Inside EIB’s critical meetings, and USAID’s controversial shutdown
item.description: For the latest episode of our podcast series, Rumbi Chakamba sits down with Michael Igoe and Jesse Chase-Lubitz to discuss the top global development stories of the week.

item.title: Are faith-based organizations the future of the AIDS response?
item.description: The new America First Global Health Strategy cuts off U.S. support to local organizations to fight HIV, except for faith-based organizations. But what happens to services for the communities faith groups cannot access?

item.title: Caribbean Civil Society Gathered in Jamaica to Strengthen Resilience Amid Global Shifts
item.description: Community groups are being heralded as the Caribbean’s cornerstone of resilience, but leaders warn they need stronger support to withstand climate shocks and growing geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: International Women’s Day 2026 No Country in the World has Reached Full Legal Equality for Women and Girls
item.description: On 8 March 2026, International Women’s Day, UN Women issues a global alert: justice systems meant to uphold rights and the rule of law are failing women and girls everywhere. Women globally hold just 64 per cent of the legal rights of men, exposing them to discrimination, violence, and exclusion at every stage of their […]
